message:
fix(command): realign FUNCTION STATS test with pinned nil running_script (br-frankenredis-j4vq)

Commit fd17b66 ("docs(command): pin FUNCTION LIST/STATS reply shape to
live redis:7.4 behavior") self-contradictorily flipped the test at
lib.rs:30435 to expect Integer(0) while the implementation at lib.rs:6394
stayed at BulkString(None) with a PINNED 2026-04-22 comment citing the
live redis:7.4.8 probe.

Fresh probe against vendored legacy_redis_code/redis (redis_version 7.2.4):

  $ redis-cli --no-raw FUNCTION STATS
  1) "running_script"
  2) (nil)
  3) "engines"
  ...

confirms the impl side and its PINNED comment are correct; the test diff
was the stray change. Restore the test assertion to BulkString(None) and
add a matching PINNED 2026-04-23 comment above the assert_eq so future
agents don't re-flip the oscillation.

Verified: fr-command --lib now 606 passed / 0 failed / 2 ignored.

Co-Authored-By: cc-redis <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -30428,11 +30428,20 @@ mod tests {
 
         let stats = dispatch_argv(&[b"FUNCTION".to_vec(), b"STATS".to_vec()], &mut store, 0)
             .expect("function stats");
+        // PINNED 2026-04-23 — running_script is RESP2 nil (BulkString(None))
+        // when no script is running, matching the implementation at
+        // lib.rs:6394 and the stock redis:7.4 / redis:7.2.4 live-oracle
+        // probe (redis-cli --no-raw FUNCTION STATS returns "(nil)" in the
+        // running_script slot on an idle server). An earlier commit
+        // (fd17b66) self-contradictorily flipped this test to Integer(0)
+        // while the impl and its PINNED comment still asserted nil; this
+        // resolves the drift by realigning the test with both. See
+        // br-frankenredis-j4vq.
         assert_eq!(
             stats,
             RespFrame::Array(Some(vec![
                 RespFrame::BulkString(Some(b"running_script".to_vec())),
-                RespFrame::Integer(0),
+                RespFrame::BulkString(None),
                 RespFrame::BulkString(Some(b"engines".to_vec())),
                 RespFrame::Array(Some(vec![
                     RespFrame::BulkString(Some(b"LUA".to_vec())),
